pi_items.metadata_value: Brown v. Board and Tinker v. Des Moines are related landmark cases
pi_items.verified_value: Both are landmark education cases addressing constitutional rights in schools, but different constitutional bases: Brown addresses Equal Protection Clause (racial segregation), Tinker addresses First Amendment (student speech)
pi_items.source: Web search results from supreme.justia.com, law.cornell.edu, uscourts.gov; Case summaries confirm different constitutional issues

pi_items.metadata_value: Gideon v. Wainwright and Miranda v. Arizona are related landmark cases
pi_items.verified_value: Strongly related: Miranda v. Arizona (1966) builds on and extends the right to counsel established in Gideon v. Wainwright (1963) to the interrogation phase. Both establish criminal defendant rights.
pi_items.source: Web search result from shmoop.com explicitly states 'The Court would build on Gideon in cases such as Miranda v. Arizona'; confirmed by supreme.justia.com and oyez.org

pi_items.metadata_value: New York Times Co. v. Sullivan and Tinker v. Des Moines are related landmark cases
pi_items.verified_value: Both are First Amendment landmark cases, but address different contexts: NYT v. Sullivan (1964) addresses press freedom and defamation of public officials with actual malice standard; Tinker (1969) addresses student speech rights with substantial disruption test
pi_items.source: Web search results from supreme.justia.com, law.cornell.edu, uscourts.gov; both confirmed as First Amendment cases but with different doctrinal standards

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic legal database with 10 injected items (5 Supreme Court cases: Brown v. Board of Education, Gideon v. Wainwright, Miranda v. Arizona, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District; and 5 law review articles). The three case pairs to be linked are: (1) Brown v. Board of Education and Tinker v. Des Moines - both landmark education/constitutional rights cases addressing different constitutional clauses (Equal Protection and First Amendment); (2) Gideon v. Wainwright and Miranda v. Arizona - strongly related cases where Miranda extends Gideon's right-to-counsel protection to the interrogation phase; (3) New York Times Co. v. Sullivan and Tinker v. Des Moines - both First Amendment landmark cases with different doctrinal focuses (press freedom/defamation and student speech). Case names in the metadata use abbreviated forms for Tinker that will match via LIKE queries.
